title: Hohenbergia nudicaulis
taxonomy: Hohenbergia nudicaulis is a species of plant in the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. This classification puts it within the order Poales and class Liliopsida. Further, it belongs to the genus Hohenbergia, which consists of several species of terrestrial bromeliads native to South America. The family Bromeliaceae includes over 3,000 species, ranging from the well-known pineapple to various epiphytes and terrestrial plants. As a member of the Bromeliaceae family, Hohenbergia nudicaulis shares characteristics with other plants in this group, such as adapted leaves for water storage.

intro: Hohenbergia nudicaulis, also known as the "false agave," is a type of succulent plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Hohenbergia nudicaulis include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin problems, fever, and digestive issues.
